frame_analyses.description: Title slide 'Setup and positioning' - instructional content begins
Expert instructor (female, CareRusher logo visible) speaking to camera in office setting
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational introduction by subject matter expert

frame_analyses.description: 3-DMed trainer box visible with Penrose drain setup on suture block; instructor hands visible holding instruments
Right needle driver approaching drain with suture needle visible; instructional demonstration of needle loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned approximately 2/3 from tip; perpendicular approach to drain

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver executing entry through Penrose drain at marked location; suture trajectory shown
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean perpendicular needle entry; mark visibility good

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting drain through second mark; suture passed completely through with good positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears well-aligned with mark; smooth needle passage

frame_analyses.description: Right hand (leading) beginning first throw wrapping motion around suture; double throw formation visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot double throw initiated; right hand leads wrap

frame_analyses.description: First throw (double wrap) complete and cinched down; knot appears secure and centered on drain slit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth cinching motion; knot positioned well; good tension

frame_analyses.description: Left hand (switched from first throw) beginning second throw; single wrap formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch executed cleanly; left hand leads second throw as required

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completed and cinched; alternating hand switching demonstrates proper technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Single throw correct; secure cinch; alternating hand pattern maintained

frame_analyses.description: Right hand (switched from second throw) beginning third throw; single wrap visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch back to right; third single throw initiated

frame_analyses.description: Third throw completed and cinched; complete knot sequence finished with all three throws secure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot appears tight and well-approximated; drain slit closure visible and complete

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic scissors visible approaching completed knot; suture tails being cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors positioned to cut both tails inside trainer as required

frame_analyses.description: Final knot with both suture tails cut; drain slit appears fully approximated; task completion achieved
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion; no visible gaps in drain slit; knot remains secure

frame_analyses.description: Instructor returning to camera view; summary of technique and common errors being discussed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational wrap-up; expert provides verbal context

note_on_performance_scoring: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL/EXPERT DEMONSTRATION video, not a trainee performance. Scoring for proficiency cannot be applied because: (1) This is a teaching video with narration, educational framing ('Setup and positioning' title slide), and expert instructor present throughout; (2) The demonstration is segmented for teaching purposes with pauses and camera work that does not reflect actual task timing; (3) The purpose is to show correct technique, not to assess a learner under timed conditions. No FLS score is computed for instructional content.
video_classification_details: This video is clearly instructional/expert_demo hybrid: presence of title slides, expert instructor on camera (CareRusher affiliation), segmented presentation of technique phases, equipment orientation, and verbal instruction indicate this is educational content designed to teach correct FLS task5 technique rather than assess trainee performance.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time — video is edited for instruction and does not represent real-time task performance
Real penalty assessment — expert demo avoids errors by design; penalties from a true performance cannot be inferred
Trainee proficiency level — this is not a trainee performance

demonstrated_techniques: Needle loading at 2/3 point from tip for optimal control
Perpendicular needle entry into Penrose drain to ensure mark alignment
Surgeon's knot (double throw) as first throw — right hand leading wrap
Alternating hand switches between throws 2 and 3 (left hand leads throw 2, right hand leads throw 3)
Single throws for throws 2 and 3 (no double wraps after surgeon's knot)
Smooth cinching technique to secure knot without excessive tension
Proper knot positioning centered on drain slit
Drain slit closure verification — no gaps visible after knot
Cutting both suture tails inside the trainer box with laparoscopic scissors
verbal_cues: Instructor discusses technique and common pitfalls (visible in summary segment)
Step-by-step phasing of suture placement, throws, and completion
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding non-perpendicular needle entry (expert shows clean perpendicular approach)
Incorrect hand switching pattern (expert demonstrates proper alternation)
Inadequate knot security (expert shows firm cinching with appropriate tension)
Leaving gaps in drain slit approximation (expert closure is complete)
Improper throw sequencing (expert clearly shows surgeon's knot first, then two single throws)
Failure to cut suture ends inside trainer (expert completes all cuts within box)

assessment_summary: This is a high-quality instructional/expert demonstration video of FLS Task 5 (Intracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying). The expert instructor demonstrates textbook-correct technique including proper needle positioning, perpendicular entry, accurate mark placement, correct surgeon's knot formation, proper hand switching across all three throws, secure knot cinching, complete drain slit approximation, and proper suture cutting. The video is structured for teaching with clear phase transitions and visual focus on technique details. As instructional content, no FLS score is computed; instead, teaching content has been extracted for reference.
confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is certain: title slides, expert instructor on camera, edited presentation format, and educational framing make it unambiguous this is not a trainee performance requiring scoring. Teaching content extraction is based on clearly visible expert technique demonstration.
